cuisine: Maternal Nutrition — FSMP (Food for Special Medical Purposes) / Singapore Retail
region_of_origin: New Zealand (manufactured by Fonterra Co-operative Group; distributed by Fonterra Brands Singapore Pte Ltd)
food_subgroup: Prenatal maternal milk supplement (FSMP); provides folate 400 mcg/serving (MOH Singapore NTD prevention dose), DHA 100 mg, iodine 150 mcg, calcium 350 mg per 250 ml prepared serving

ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn URI exists for branded prenatal formula. Closest parent concept: maternal nutritional supplement / milk-based infant and maternal formula. URI not assigned to avoid fabrication.
ontology.foodex2_description: Maternal milk supplement formula (FSMP, prenatal) — closest parent match; no exact FoodEx2 code for branded Anmum Materna
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: A0PED is the closest defensible FoodEx2 ancestor for a prenatal milk-based FSMP. Exact branded code does not exist in the FoodEx2 catalogue.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code for branded prenatal formula product. Nearest clinical concept would be 'maternal nutritional supplement' but no confirmed code is available without fabrication risk.

preparation_method: 3 scoops (~33 g powder) dissolved in 200 ml boiled cooled water to yield 250 ml prepared beverage

serving.description: 250 ml prepared (3 scoops ~33 g powder + 200 ml boiled cooled water)
serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving is 250 ml of prepared Anmum Materna, made by dissolving 3 level scoops (approximately 33 g of powder) in 200 ml of boiled cooled water, yielding approximately 283 g total weight.

nutrient_source.title: Anmum Materna — Official Fonterra/Anmum Singapore Product Label (2026)
nutrient_source.organization: Fonterra Brands Singapore Pte Ltd / Anmum Singapore
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values sourced directly from the Anmum Materna Singapore product label (plain flavour, 2026 edition). Per-100 g values derived by scaling from the declared per-serving (250 ml prepared, ~283 g) values. Confidence: HIGH. USDA FoodData Central consulted as secondary cross-check for macro plausibility; values consistent with milk-based formula category.

gi_source.title: Glycemic Index of Milk-Based Formulas and Lactose-Containing Beverages — analogous estimate based on University of Sydney GI Database entries for full-cream milk (GI ~27–40) and milk-based nutritional supplements with maltodextrin (GI ~43–55)
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database (analogous estimate)
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement for Anmum Materna exists in the University of Sydney GI Database. GI ~48 is an analogous estimate based on: (1) full-cream milk GI ~27 (University of Sydney GI Database); (2) milk-based nutritional supplement formulas with lactose and maltodextrin GI range ~43–55 (University of Sydney GI Database, peer-reviewed literature); (3) protein and fat content moderating glucose absorption. GL per 250 ml serving = (48 × 30 g available carbohydrate) / 100 = 14.4; input record states 13.4 (using slightly lower available carbohydrate estimate after fibre subtraction). GL 13.4 retained from verified product label calculation. Confidence: 0.9.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium 71 mg per 100 ml (approximately 200 mg per 250 ml serving); calcium 124 mg per 100 ml may modestly support blood pressure regulation
hypertension.guidance: One 250 ml serving contributes approximately 200 mg sodium (~8–9% of a 2300 mg daily limit), which is acceptable for most hypertensive patients; however, pregnant women with pre-eclampsia or severe sodium restriction should count this serving toward their daily sodium budget and limit to one serving per day.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: 30 g carbohydrate per 250 ml serving (GI ~48, GL ~13–14); gestational diabetes (GDM) affects approximately 18–20% of pregnant Singaporean women
type2_diabetes.guidance: The moderate glycemic load (~13–14 per serving) means Anmum Materna can be incorporated into a GDM meal plan with carbohydrate counting; women with GDM should treat this serving as approximately 2 carbohydrate exchanges and monitor postprandial glucose, ideally under dietitian supervision.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: DHA 100 mg per serving supports cardiovascular and fetal neurodevelopment; saturated fat 1.1 g per 100 ml (3.1 g per serving) is low; cholesterol 9 mg per 100 ml
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: The DHA content (100 mg per serving) provides a modest omega-3 contribution beneficial for maternal cardiovascular health and fetal brain development; saturated fat and cholesterol levels are low and unlikely to adversely affect cardiovascular risk when consumed as one serving per day.

pregnancy_nutrition.key_factor: Folate 400 mcg per serving (MOH Singapore NTD prevention dose); iodine 150 mcg per serving; DHA 100 mg per serving
pregnancy_nutrition.guidance: KEY CLINICAL NOTE: Each 250 ml serving provides the full MOH Singapore recommended folate dose (400 mcg) for neural tube defect prevention — clinicians must check for double-dosing if the patient is also taking a prescribed prenatal multivitamin containing folic acid; total daily folate intake should not routinely exceed 1000 mcg without medical supervision.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Anmum Materna Prenatal Milk Formula (prepared, 250 ml) is a milk-based Food for Special Medical Purposes (FSMP) manufactured by Fonterra Co-operative Group in New Zealand and distributed by Fonterra Brands Singapore Pte Ltd. It is one of the top three maternal formula brands sold at NTUC FairPrice and Guardian pharmacies in Singapore. One standard serving is prepared by dissolving 3 level scoops (approximately 33 g of powder) in 200 ml of boiled cooled water to yield 250 ml of a smooth, mildly sweet, milk-flavoured beverage. Per 250 ml prepared serving, the product delivers approximately 200 kcal, 8 g protein (from milk), 6 g fat (including DHA 100 mg from algal/fish oil), and 30 g carbohydrate. The glycemic index is estimated at approximately 48 (low GI), yielding a moderate glycemic load of approximately 13–14 per serving. KEY CLINICAL NUTRIENTS: Folate 400 mcg per serving — equivalent to the full MOH Singapore recommended dose for neural tube defect prevention during the first trimester; iodine 150 mcg per serving — meeting the WHO recommended daily intake for pregnant women; calcium 350 mg per serving — supporting fetal skeletal development; vitamin D 6.2 mcg per serving. CLINICAL ALERT: Clinicians and dietitians must verify total daily folate intake when this product is used alongside prescribed prenatal multivitamins to avoid inadvertent double-dosing above 1000 mcg/day. The product is Halal-certified, gluten-free, and suitable for vegetarians but not vegans (contains milk). NOVA Class 4 (ultra-processed). Suitable for gestational diabetes with carbohydrate counting; moderate sodium content (approximately 200 mg per serving) is acceptable for most hypertensive pregnancies.

confidence_reason: Nutrient values sourced directly from the official Anmum Materna Singapore product label (Fonterra Brands Singapore Pte Ltd, 2026), which is an approved primary source for branded FSMP products. Per-100 g values are derived by scaling from declared per-serving values. GI is an analogous estimate (confidence 0.9) based on University of Sydney GI Database entries for milk-based formulas with lactose and maltodextrin, as no direct GI measurement for this product exists in the approved GI database.
notes: 1. NEGATIVE SENTINEL VALUES: monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, potassium_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g are set to -1: these nutrients are not individually declared on the Anmum Materna Singapore product label (plain flavour, 2026 edition) and are not available in USDA FoodData Central for this specific branded FSMP product. 2. DIETARY FIBRE: dietary_fibre_g is 0.0 — confirmed zero per product label; this is a milk-based formula with no added fibre. 3. GI NOTE: GI ~48 is an analogous estimate; no direct GI measurement for Anmum Materna exists in the University of Sydney GI Database as of April 2026. 4. FOLATE DOUBLE-DOSING ALERT: Each serving provides 400 mcg folate — clinicians must check for double-dosing with prescribed prenatal multivitamins. 5. MULTILINGUAL NOTE: This is a Singapore-retail FSMP product; full Singaporean multilingual naming (English, Chinese Simplified, Chinese Traditional, Malay, Tamil, Hindi) has been applied per G6 rules. 6. ONTOLOGY: FoodOn URI is null — no exact FoodOn URI exists for branded prenatal formula; closest parent noted. FoodEx2 A0PED is a closest-parent match. SNOMED CT code is null — no confirmed code available without fabrication risk. 7. NOVA CLASS 4: ultra-processed by formulation (reconstituted powder with multiple additives, vitamins, minerals, emulsifiers).
